title: Exclusive Time of Functions
hints: Notice that functions are properly nested like parentheses, suggesting a last-in-first-out structure to track the currently executing function.
Use a stack to keep track of active function calls and maintain a variable for the previous timestamp to calculate elapsed time.

At every log event, the function currently running (the innermost active one) has earned all the time since the previous event — credit it before the set of active functions changes. The subtlety is that a start and an end treat the boundary timestamp differently, since an 'end' includes the whole of its own time unit while a 'start' begins one; advance your 'last event' marker to match.
